evals.prompt: Write release notes for v1.2.0 using these merged PR summaries: fix login redirect, add CSV export, update docs.
evals.expected_output: Routes to the single Release Notes standard, not the Version Release workflow pack. Output contains highlights, changes, upgrade notes only when evidenced, and no publishing action.

evals.prompt: Prepare the full v1.2.0 release materials for this repository.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Version Release. Asks one package-selection question with three options and a recommended default, includes optional supplements, defaults to local .github-writing/version-release/v1.2.0/ drafts, and includes package-manifest.md.

evals.prompt: Write a README for this agent skill repository.
evals.expected_output: Uses the README three-choice prompt unless the user skips questions. README output includes applicability scope, install or usage, file index, evidence-bounded support claims, and no links to nonexistent README language files.

evals.prompt: I have a PR postmortem. Rewrite it as a feature request for future work, not as a PR description.
evals.expected_output: Routes by requested output to Feature Request. Does not produce a PR description. Includes motivation, use cases, proposed behavior, alternatives, compatibility, and open questions as evidence allows.

evals.prompt: Create a GitHub bug report from this messy note: app crashes after clicking Export, expected CSV download, actual blank page, Chrome 125, macOS, logs attached.
evals.expected_output: Produces only the GitHub bug report artifact. No chat preface, no outer markdown fence, no test title, no unrelated code block, and no invented root cause.

evals.prompt: Create a GitHub Issue Form YAML for reporting bugs. I have not told you our label names.
evals.expected_output: Produces valid GitHub issue-form YAML and omits labels, projects, assignees, SIGs, version lists, and required fields that are not evidenced.

evals.prompt: Write a feature PR description for adding CSV export to reports. I have not run tests.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Feature PR and marks testing as not run instead of claiming verification.

evals.prompt: Review this diff and lead with actionable findings before summary.
evals.expected_output: Routes to Code Review. Findings appear before summary, are severity-labeled, and avoid invented line numbers or tests.
